Directed by Zhang Yimou, known for films like Гўв‚¬Е“House of Flying DaggersГўв‚¬Вќ and for orchestrating the opening and closing ceremonies of the 2008 Summer Olympic Games in Beijing, Гўв‚¬Е“The Great WallГўв‚¬Вќ finds Mr. Damon in ancient China as William Garin, a bedraggled European mercenary. U.N. special envoy on Syria Staffan de Mistura will arrive in Kazakhstan s capital on Friday to take part in Syria peace talks, the Kazakh Foreign Ministry said on Thursday, according to Russia s state news agency RIA. European Council President Donald Tusk said on Tuesday that it was up to Britain to determine if there was to be a good deal or no deal in Brexit talks.  We have managed to build and maintain unity among the 27, but ahead of us is still the toughest stress test. If we fail it, the negotiations will end in our defeat. We must keep our unity regardless of the direction of the talks. The EU will be able to rise to every scenario as long as we are not divided,  Tusk, who chairs summits of EU leaders, told the European Parliament.  It is in fact up to London how this will end, with a good deal, no deal or no Brexit but in each of these scenarios we will protect our common interests only by being together,  he said.

Donald Trump is running riot in the GOP china shop and gleefully tearing the place up.

Consider the strength of TrumpГўв‚¬в„ўs position: If he wins South Carolina by a big margin, he goes into Nevada with momentum, and the latest poll there has him leading by 26 points and pushing 50 percent. If he enters Super Tuesday a week later having won three out of the past three states Гўв‚¬вЂќ and with Cruz diminished by a South Carolina loss and Rubio having won nowhere Гўв‚¬вЂќ he could easily win, say, 10 contests that day.

It might still be possible to beat Trump at that point if the field is narrowed (states donГўв‚¬в„ўt become winner-take-all until March 15), but doing so would involve wrestling to the ground a candidate who will have cut a formidable swath through the first month of the nomination battle.

Even now, it's hard to imagine a happy outcome for the party from the three likeliest scenarios:

Гўв‚¬вЂќ If Trump wins the nomination outright, many Republican voters may stay home, and senators and members of the House up for reelection will probably scurry their own way, seeking cover from the loose cannon of a nominee.

It is certainly possible that Trump will prove a better general election candidate than expected, just as he has proved a much more potent candidate in the primary than nearly anyone thought (and he will presumably be facing a very weak Hillary Clinton). But it is also likely that the general public will be less enamored or forgiving of those qualities in Trump that have charmed or at least not bothered a plurality of the Republican electorate Гўв‚¬вЂќ the lack of political experience, the foul mouth, the constant psychodrama, the spotty business record. Surely, the first Democratic ads against him will portray him as a Гўв‚¬Е“vulture capitalistГўв‚¬Вќ like Mitt Romney, except without the manners.

Гўв‚¬вЂќ If Trump is dragged to an open convention and leads in delegates, but falls short of a majority, and is denied the nomination, there will be a bloodbath. Trump will make Andrew JacksonГўв‚¬в„ўs angry cry of a Гўв‚¬Е“corrupt bargainГўв‚¬Вќ after Old Hickory lost the presidency in the House of Representatives in 1824 Гўв‚¬вЂќ despite leading in popular and electoral votes Гўв‚¬вЂќ look like a measured, cool-headed response. Trump will stomp off, and no doubt take a lot of his supporters with him.

Гўв‚¬вЂќ If Trump is beaten prior to a convention, it will presumably require an all-out war against the mogul. Well-heeled Republican donors will have to pour money into an thermonuclear advertising campaign to destroy his image. The party will have to rally around a Trump alternative, doing everything in its power to bolster him and tear down Trump. Such an effort will no doubt strike Trump as Гўв‚¬Е“unfair,Гўв‚¬Вќ and he will do all he can to delegitimize it and find targets to sue over it. Needless to say, none of this would be conducive to keeping Trump voters inside the Republican tent.

Has any political party ever had a candidate who is such a wrecking ball, and who isnГўв‚¬в„ўt a fringe candidate, but a dominant one?

The Republican front-runner is threatening to sue one of his challengers, Ted Cruz (who is now daring Trump to file suit and saying he would relish the prospect of himself questioning Trump under oath Гўв‚¬вЂќ this is really happening). The Republican front-runner thinks the last Republican president was guilty of impeachable offenses and lied the country into war. The Republican front-runner routinely attacks his own party for its perfidy Гўв‚¬вЂќ he claims the Iowa caucuses were stolen from him and the debate audiences are stacked against him Гўв‚¬вЂќ and insults his competitors in the harshest, most personal terms.

WeГўв‚¬в„ўve grown used to how Trump has treated Jeb Bush in the debates, but that doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt make it any less appalling a breach of political norms or basic decency. The faces he makes while Bush talks, the constant interrupting, the petty put-downs Гўв‚¬вЂќ all of this would have been thought unworthy of the lowest political guttersnipe but have become an accepted part of the landscape thanks to Donald J. Trump.

And no one has figured out a way to handle TrumpГўв‚¬в„ўs bullying. Bush has gotten stronger on stage but has never put Trump in his place. Even Ted Cruz has shrunk before Trump at key moments Гўв‚¬вЂќ for instance, painfully in the New Hampshire debate, when he refused to repeat a critique of TrumpГўв‚¬в„ўs temperament, prompting the mogul to comment, Гўв‚¬Е“people back down with Trump.Гўв‚¬Вќ The Donald has a way of making needling, Гўв‚¬Е“when did you stop beating your wife?Гўв‚¬Вќ attacks that are inherently difficult to counter. When he stands next to Ted Cruz and says no one likes him, how is the senator supposed to respond? Гўв‚¬Е“No, Donald, let me assure you, I really do have friendsГўв‚¬Вќ?

The key to TrumpГўв‚¬в„ўs strength, which buttresses all his outrageousness, is that his supporters want someone to blow up the system. So there's almost nothing he can say or do that will discredit him in their eyes, and the least destructive scenario for his defeat Гўв‚¬вЂќ Trump blows himself up Гўв‚¬вЂќ will take some doing on his part.

ItГўв‚¬в„ўs all very entertaining Гўв‚¬вЂќ but so are demolition derbies.
